title: psephellus hymenolepis
intro: Psephellus hymenolepis, also known as the Golden Ragwort or Pyrethrum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Psephellus hymenolepis include its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions and wound healing. Psephellus hymenolepis can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used topically to promote healing and reduce pain.
